revision.text:
[[ja:鷲山寺]] [[ja:鷲山寺]] [[ja:鷲山寺]] [[ja:鷲山寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Jusenji20120819.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Mobara|qid=Q502486|1=Mobara}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42875|N|140.282333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Sanbōson|qid=Q11355123|1=Sanbōson}}}} {{Temple in Japan |Name = Jusenji |image = [[File:jusenji20120819.jpg|236px]]
Main Hall |Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mobara|en|Mobara|qid=Q502486|lt=Mobara|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|Washinosu (Mobara)|ja|鷲巣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinosu (Mobara)|1=Q131227554|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131227554}} 48 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|43.5|N|140|16|56.4|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=it}} |Map = Japan Chiba |Mountain Name = Chōkokuzan |Sect = {{ill|Hokkeshu Hommonryu|ja|法華宗本門流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkeshu Hommonryu|1=Hokke-shū Honmon-ryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555316}} |Temple Rank = Daihonzan |Main image = {{ill|Sanbōson|ja|三宝尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbōson|1=Sanbōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355123}} |Founded = 1277 (Kenji 3) |Founder = {{ill|Nichiben|ja|日弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiben|1=Nichiben|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505310}} |Patron = Kobayakawa Naiki }} '''Jusenji''' (鷲山寺) is a {{ill|Daihonzan|ja|大本山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihonzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本山|1=honzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24887668}} of the {{ill|Hokkeshu Hommonryu|ja|法華宗本門流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkeshu Hommonryu|1=Hokke-shū Honmon-ryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555316}} sect, located in {{ill|Washinosu (Mobara)|ja|鷲巣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washinosu (Mobara)|1=Q131227554|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131227554}}, {{ill|Mobara|en|Mobara|qid=Q502486|lt=Mobara|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It has two sub-temples (Tatchu): Kanmō-in and Myōhon-ji. [[File:Jusenji_miei.jpg|240px|thumb|"Statue of Nichiren Daishōnin" (Traditionally attributed to Nichihō Shōnin)]] == Overview == Since its founding, Jusenji has suffered four major fires that destroyed its buildings. The current structures include the Main Hall, Kuri (residential quarters), Founder’s Hall, Bell Tower Hall, Suigyō Hall (ritual purification hall), Niōmon Gate, Kōshin Hall, Nagaya Gate, and other facilities, although many are temporary. The temple grounds also contain a memorial for the victims of the {{ill|Genroku Earthquake|ja|元禄大地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku Earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元禄地震|1=1703 Genroku earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q770728}} tsunami. The current chief priest is the 102nd abbot, Sato NichikenAppointed from {{ill|Shinryuji|ja|信隆寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinryuji|1=Q30319298|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30319298}} in Katsushika Ward on June 11, 2016.. == History == * 1264 (Bunei 1): {{ill|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|qid=Q31147812=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|comment=no wikipedias work|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qq=}} receives the patronage of Kobayakawa Naiki. * 1277 (Kenji 3): {{ill|Nichiben|ja|日弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiben|1=Nichiben|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505310}}, with the support of Kobayakawa Naiki, establishes Jusenji. * 1535 (Tenmon 4): A fire destroys the temple, which is later rebuilt. * 1705 (Hōei 2): Another fire destroys the temple, which is again rebuilt. * 1862 (Bunkyū 2): A fire destroys the temple, which is subsequently rebuilt. * 1954 (Shōwa 29): Fireworks sponsored by the city of Mobara cause a fire that burns down the Main Hall. == Cultural Properties == * Genroku Tsunami Memorial Tower (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Mobara City) == Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shishiku-kai|ja|獅子吼会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiku-kai|1=Q11572195|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572195}} * {{ill|Washijinja|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Washijinja|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11676283}} (separated during {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}) * {{ill|Yōgyōji (Ōamishirasato)|ja|要行寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgyōji (Ōamishirasato)|1=Q11630065|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630065}} (Chūhonzan of Hokkeshu Hommonryu) {{translated page|ja|鷲山寺|version=102470717|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鷲山寺]] {{wikidata link|Q11676248}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Mobara, Chiba]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Temples of the Hokkeshū Honmon Ryū]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
